existing_Entity_Description: Kent is a municipality in Fraser Valley, British Columbia, Canada, within the Fraser Valley. As of the 2021 Canadian Census, Kent had a population of 6,300. The community is situated in fertile farmland meets suburban growth in the shadow of the Coast Mountains. one of BC's fastest-growing regions with rising service demand. The region features rainy but mild winters with warm, pleasant summers.

• Distance from Vancouver: ~99 km

REGION_BUNDLE.climate: Coastal climate with more temperature range than Metro Vancouver: heavier winter rain, occasional outflow freezes and snow events, hot summers; the November 2021 atmospheric-river flooding of the Sumas Prairie made valley drainage a household topic.
REGION_BUNDLE.terrain: Broad, fertile Fraser River floodplain framed by the Cascade and Coast mountains; Sumas Prairie reclaimed lakebed; steep slopes above Chilliwack and Hope.
REGION_BUNDLE.economy: BC's agricultural heartland: dairy, berries (Abbotsford calls itself the Raspberry Capital of Canada), poultry; plus aerospace at Abbotsford International Airport, manufacturing, and rapid residential growth along Highway 1.
REGION_BUNDLE.building_stock: Farmhouses and rural acreages, big post-2000 suburban subdivisions in Abbotsford and Chilliwack, and new multi-family districts around urban centres; many properties on well and septic outside city cores.
